subject_tmpl: Parkway tree trimming needed at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. The parkway tree out front hasn't been trimmed in years and the branches are hanging low enough to scrape car roofs. A couple of them look like they might break in the next storm. Can someone come out and take a look? I've been patient but this has been going on for over {DAYS} days now. Thanks.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for reaching out. We have logged a trimming request for the parkway tree at {ADDRESS} and will schedule a crew visit within the next {DAYS} days. We appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: Dead tree at {ADDRESS} — safety concern
body_tmpl: To whom it may concern, the large tree on the parkway in front of {ADDRESS} appears to be completely dead. There are no leaves on it at all even though it's {season} and the bark is falling off in big chunks. It leans slightly toward the street and I'm worried it could fall on a car or a kid walking by. Please have someone remove it as soon as possible.

subject_tmpl: Storm debris / downed limbs on {STREET}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here at {ADDRESS}. After last night's storm there are three large limbs down in the parkway and one is blocking part of the road near {CROSS}. Drivers are swerving around it. I called the non-emergency line but they said to email Public Works. Can you send someone today?
reply_tmpl: Thank you {FIRST}. A crew has been dispatched to {STREET} near {CROSS} and will clear the debris. For urgent storm damage please also call us directly at the Public Works main line.

subject_tmpl: Tree roots lifting sidewalk on {STREET} — trip hazard
body_tmpl: Good morning. I wanted to flag a serious trip hazard on the sidewalk along {STREET} near {CROSS}. The parkway tree roots have pushed up two panels of concrete by about two inches. My neighbor at {ADDRESS} nearly fell last week and my mother uses a walker. This has been an issue for {DAYS} days and I'd really like to know what the Village plans to do about it.
reply_tmpl: We appreciate you reporting this, {FIRST}. We have added the location at {STREET} and {CROSS} to our sidewalk repair queue. A concrete crew will evaluate the root damage and recommend whether the tree needs to be removed as part of the repair.

subject_tmpl: Ash tree on parkway — possible EAB
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm reaching out because I'm concerned that the parkway ash tree at {ADDRESS} may have emerald ash borer. I've noticed a lot of woodpecker activity, bark splitting near the base, and S-shaped galleries under some of the loose bark. I know EAB has been a problem in this area. Is the Village doing any inspections? I don't want to lose this tree if treatment is still an option.

subject_tmpl: Please help — dangerous leaning tree at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've emailed Public Works twice about the large oak on the parkway at {ADDRESS} that is leaning toward my house. I first wrote on {DATE} and have not received a response. This tree is enormous and I am afraid for my family. I am asking you personally to intervene and get someone out here. I pay my taxes and I expect the Village to respond. Thank you.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for writing. I have personally asked Public Works Director DiMeo to schedule an inspection at {ADDRESS} this week. You will be contacted by {DATE} with a plan of action. I take this seriously.

subject_tmpl: Overhanging branches blocking streetlight on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello, the parkway tree near {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has branches that have grown over the streetlight and it's very dark on that block now. I walk my dog after work and I can barely see. I'd like to request that the branches over the light be trimmed back. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Thank you for the tree removal on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I just wanted to say thank you. The crew came out and removed the dead elm in front of {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. They cleaned up everything and even raked the wood chips off my lawn. It's so refreshing to see the Village follow through promptly. Please pass my thanks to the Public Works team.
reply_tmpl: Thank you so much for sharing that, {FIRST}. I'll make sure Frank and the crew hear your kind words. Taking care of Bellwood's trees is something we care about deeply.

subject_tmpl: Stump grinding request — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. The Village removed a parkway tree here about three months ago but the stump is still sitting there. It's hard to mow around and honestly looks bad. When does stump grinding happen? Is there a schedule I should know about, or do I need to put in a separate request?
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, stump removal is typically scheduled on a separate cycle from tree removal. Your address is now on the grinding list and we expect to reach {STREET} within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Parkway tree branches over power lines at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. I live at {ADDRESS} and the parkway tree out front has grown into the power lines. I'm nervous every time the wind picks up. I know ComEd sometimes handles this but I wasn't sure who to call first — the Village or the utility. The branches look like they could pull down the line. Can you let me know who is responsible?

subject_tmpl: Winter storm downed a large limb on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm {NAME} and I live on {STREET}. The ice storm on {DATE} brought down a very large limb from the parkway tree in front of {ADDRESS}. The limb fell across the sidewalk and part of it is in the street. I've put up some orange cones I had in my garage but it really needs to be cleared. Please send someone as soon as you can.
reply_tmpl: Thank you {NAME}. We have your request and a crew will be out to {STREET} to clear the limb. Winter storm response is our priority right now but we'll get there as soon as possible.

subject_tmpl: Request to plant a replacement parkway tree at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, the Village removed the parkway tree in front of my house at {ADDRESS} last spring and it has not been replaced. The bare patch looks bad and I actually miss the shade in the summer. Is there a program to replant parkway trees? If so, how do I get on the list? I'd prefer something that won't have root problems like the last one. Thanks, {FIRST}.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor's tree overhanging my property at {STREET}
body_tmpl: I'm {FIRST} {LAST} at {ADDRESS}. My neighbor's tree has branches extending well over my property line, dropping debris in my gutters and on my car in the driveway. I've asked them twice to trim it and nothing has happened. Is this something Code Enforcement handles or do I need to hire my own arborist? I don't want a dispute with my neighbor but enough is enough.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, branches overhanging from private property to private property are generally a civil matter between neighbors. However, if the tree poses a hazard, we can send a code officer to assess whether a notice of violation is warranted. We'll have someone contact you.

subject_tmpl: EAB — ash trees on {STREET} showing decline
body_tmpl: Hi, this is {FIRST} {LAST}, a longtime resident on {STREET}. I've been watching the row of parkway ash trees on our block and at least four of them are showing thinning crowns and epicormic sprouting — classic EAB signs. I contacted the Morton Arboretum and they confirmed the description matches. The Village should do a formal assessment before we lose them all. Can you coordinate with a certified arborist?

subject_tmpl: Tree debris clogging my drain — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: The parkway tree at {ADDRESS} drops tremendous amounts of seed pods and leaves every fall and they clog my catch basin. I end up doing the clearing myself but after last night's rain the street flooded in front of my house. I know the tree is Village property. Can you schedule more frequent debris removal on {STREET} during {season}? This is an annual problem.
reply_tmpl: We understand your frustration, {FIRST}. We'll add {STREET} to the fall street sweeping priority list and have Public Works inspect the catch basin at {ADDRESS}. Thank you for letting us know.

subject_tmpl: FOIA request — Village forestry/tree maintenance records
body_tmpl: Dear Village Clerk, I am submitting a FOIA request for all records related to parkway tree maintenance, removal, and planting on {STREET} between {DATE} and {DATE}. This would include work orders, contractor invoices, inspection reports, and any communications with residents. Please advise on the timeframe and any associated fees. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, we have received your FOIA request dated {DATE}. Under Illinois law you will receive a response within 5 business days. We will contact you if a fee estimate is needed.

subject_tmpl: Referral: hazardous parkway tree — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Frank, I was doing a code sweep on {STREET} today and noticed the parkway tree at {ADDRESS} has significant trunk decay at the base — looks like a cavity you could fit your arm into. I issued a note to the file but this is really a PW/forestry issue. Can you get someone out to assess before we get a liability complaint? I can send photos if helpful.
reply_tmpl: Sandra, thanks for the heads-up. I'll put it on the inspection board for this week. Send the photos to my email and I'll attach them to the work order.

subject_tmpl: Tree root impact on stormwater infrastructure — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Frank — during our storm sewer camera inspection on {STREET} near {CROSS} we found root intrusion in two sections of pipe. The parkway trees in that block are old silver maples and the roots are significant. We'll need to coordinate removal or root barrier installation before we can line the pipe. Let me know what the forestry assessment shows and we can plan the capital work together.

subject_tmpl: EAB situation update — Village ash tree inventory
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I wanted to give you an update on the emerald ash borer situation. We have completed an inventory of Village-owned ash trees and identified {NUM} trees showing moderate to severe decline. My recommendation is to begin a phased removal program starting with the {NUM} highest-risk trees on {STREET} and {CROSS}. Budget estimate is attached. I'd like to discuss at the next staff meeting.
reply_tmpl: Frank, thank you. Let's schedule time before the board meeting on {DATE} to go over the numbers. I want to be able to give the trustees a full picture.

subject_tmpl: Trimming request — branches rubbing my roof at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: My name is {FIRST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. The parkway tree branches are rubbing against my roof every time the wind blows and I'm worried about shingle damage. My roofer said the branches need to be cut back at least six feet from the house. Since it's a Village tree I figured I should contact you first before doing anything myself. Please advise.

subject_tmpl: Fallen tree blocking alley near {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, there is a tree that fell across the alley behind {ADDRESS} on {STREET} last night. I think it might be Village property — it was growing in the parkway on the other side of the fence. Garbage trucks can't get through and neither can my car. I need this resolved quickly. Please send a crew today if possible. This is {FIRST} {LAST}, you can reach me at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we've contacted our crew and they are en route to the alley near {ADDRESS}. Thank you for the phone number — we may follow up if we need clarification on the location.

subject_tmpl: Large dead limb hanging over {ADDRESS} — urgent
body_tmpl: I need someone at {ADDRESS} as soon as possible. A dead limb roughly {AGE} feet long is still hanging in the parkway tree after the storm — it's what arborists call a widow-maker. It's directly above where my kids play and the driveway where we park. I don't feel safe. This is {NAME}, {PHONE}. Please treat this as urgent.
reply_tmpl: We understand, {NAME}. Safety is our top priority. A crew has been alerted and will respond to {ADDRESS} today. Please keep the area beneath the limb clear until it is removed.

subject_tmpl: Tree trimming schedule for {STREET} — when is our block?
body_tmpl: Hi there, I've been hearing that Public Works is doing parkway tree trimming around Bellwood this summer. Do you have a schedule posted somewhere? I'd like to know when {STREET} between {CROSS} and Washington Blvd is on the list. I want to make sure my car is moved if they're doing the block. Thanks, {FIRST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, trimming on {STREET} is currently scheduled for the week of {DATE}. We recommend keeping vehicles off the parkway side during that time. Watch for posted notices the day before.

subject_tmpl: Parkway tree roots cracking my driveway apron — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: The parkway tree at my property at {ADDRESS} has roots that have cracked and lifted my driveway apron significantly. I had to repair it last year at my own expense and it's happening again. I've read that if Village tree roots damage private property the Village may have some responsibility. Can you look into this? I'd like to discuss options before I spend another {AMOUNT} on repairs.

subject_tmpl: Whole block of ash trees dying on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I've lived on {STREET} for {AGE} years and I've watched the ash trees on our block die one by one over the past three summers. It's heartbreaking and also scary from a safety standpoint. We have {NUM} dead or near-dead trees and only one has been removed. I'd like the Village to develop a real plan for this block and communicate it to residents. Can you help?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for writing, {FIRST}. I share your concern and I've asked Public Works to prioritize {STREET} in our EAB assessment. We'll hold a brief community update — I'll have my office reach out to schedule it.

subject_tmpl: Sap and seed pods from parkway tree damaging car
body_tmpl: Hi, I park on the street in front of {ADDRESS} and the parkway tree there drops so much sap and seed pods that my car is perpetually stained. I've had to get the paint professionally cleaned twice this year at {AMOUNT} a pop. I know you probably can't do much about the sap but is there any possibility of trimming the overhanging branches or eventually replacing this species with something less messy? Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Request: prune parkway tree blocking stop sign view — {STREET} & {CROSS}
body_tmpl: I wanted to flag a safety issue at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS}. The parkway tree on the northwest corner has grown to the point where it partially obstructs the stop sign when approaching from the south. I've almost missed it a couple of times because the foliage is so dense. Can Public Works trim it back before someone gets hurt?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for this safety report. We have asked our crew to prioritize sight-line trimming at {STREET} and {CROSS} this week. We'll also alert the traffic safety coordinator.

subject_tmpl: Appreciate the quick response on {STREET} tree removal
body_tmpl: Hi Frank, this is {FIRST} {LAST} from {ADDRESS}. I emailed about the dead tree out front on {DATE} and your crew was here by {DATE} — very impressed. They were professional, cleaned up completely, and even checked in with me before they left. I'll definitely recommend neighbors contact the Village directly when they have tree concerns. Keep up the great work.

subject_tmpl: Parkway tree split during storm — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here. The parkway tree at {ADDRESS} split right down the middle last night during the storm. Half of it is still standing and half is on the parkway. No one was hurt, thankfully. I took photos. The part that's down is not blocking the road but it's a mess and the standing half looks unstable. Please advise on next steps.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, please send us the photos at the Public Works email. A foreman will review them and we'll dispatch an assessment crew to {ADDRESS}. The standing portion will be evaluated for removal.

subject_tmpl: Mushrooms at base of parkway tree — is it diseased?
body_tmpl: Hi, I noticed a large cluster of mushrooms growing at the base of the parkway tree in front of {ADDRESS}. I've read that this can be a sign of internal rot. The tree is quite large and is right next to my house. Can a Village arborist take a look? I'm not sure if this is serious or not but I'd feel better having someone assess it. I'm {FIRST}, you can reach me at {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, fungal growth at a tree base can indeed indicate root or trunk decay. We'll have our forestry inspector visit {ADDRESS} and will call you at {PHONE} to confirm a time.

subject_tmpl: Tree trimming left huge mess at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, the Village crew trimmed the parkway tree at {ADDRESS} on {DATE} and I appreciate that but they left a significant pile of wood chips and small branches on my front lawn. I'm not sure if I'm supposed to remove them or if a cleanup crew comes back. It's been {DAYS} days and the pile is still there. Can someone please come pick it up?
reply_tmpl: We apologize for the delay, {FIRST}. Our cleanup crew will return to {ADDRESS} within {DAYS} days to remove the remaining debris. Thank you for your patience.

subject_tmpl: Parkway tree damaging signage — {BIZ} at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: This is {NAME} from {BIZ} located at {ADDRESS}. The parkway tree branches are growing directly into our exterior signage and we had to reattach a panel last week. We've had this property for {AGE} years and this tree has really outgrown its space. Can the Village trim it back or evaluate whether it should be removed? We're concerned about the next storm season.

subject_tmpl: Storm limb damage to {BIZ} awning — seeking Village response
body_tmpl: Good morning. I am the owner of {BIZ} at {ADDRESS} on St. Charles Rd. During the storm on {DATE} a large limb from the Village parkway tree broke off and tore our front awning. Repair cost is approximately {AMOUNT}. Since this was a Village tree I'd like to understand the process for filing a property damage claim with the Village. Who do I contact? Thank you, {NAME}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {NAME}, we are sorry to hear about the damage to your awning at {ADDRESS}. Please contact the Village Manager's office to begin a liability claim. They will direct you to the appropriate insurance documentation process.

subject_tmpl: Woodpecker damage to ash tree — EAB concern at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, there has been intense woodpecker activity on the parkway ash tree at {ADDRESS} for the past few weeks. I've read that woodpeckers target trees infested with EAB larvae. The bark on the upper trunk is riddled with holes. I'd hate to lose this tree — it's old and beautiful — but I'd rather know the truth now. Can someone from the Village inspect it?

subject_tmpl: Root barrier coordination needed — {STREET} tree removal
body_tmpl: Tom, we're planning to remove {NUM} silver maples on {STREET} between {CROSS} and Rice Ave over the next {DAYS} days. Before we grind the stumps I want to make sure you don't need access to the area for any utility work. Also, please advise if we need root barriers installed for the replacement plantings near the storm sewer inlets. Let me know your schedule so we can coordinate.
reply_tmpl: Frank, no conflicts on my end for that stretch. Hold off on grinding the stump at the {CROSS} end — we may need to excavate there for the catch basin repair in Q{NUM}. I'll send you the drawings.

subject_tmpl: Concern: tree trimming disturbed birds nesting — {STREET}
body_tmpl: I want to report that a crew was trimming trees on {STREET} on {DATE} and in the process they disturbed what appeared to be an active bird nest in one of the branches. I'm not sure if this is illegal under migratory bird protections but I was upset to see it. I don't want to cause trouble but I think Village crews should check for nests before trimming in spring. Is there a policy for this?
reply_tmpl: Thank you for raising this, {FIRST}. Illinois and federal rules do protect active migratory bird nests. We will remind crews to check for nesting activity before trimming during nesting season. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention.

subject_tmpl: Ice-loaded branches snapped off overnight — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Last night's freezing rain brought down several branches from the parkway tree at {ADDRESS}. Two are in the street and one is on my car — it left a dent in the hood. I've already filed an auto claim but I also want to document this with the Village. The tree has had a lot of dead wood in it for a while. This feels like it could have been prevented. Please get someone out here today.
reply_tmpl: We are sorry about the damage to your vehicle, {FIRST}. We'll have a crew clear the debris at {ADDRESS} today. To document the incident with the Village, please also contact the Village Manager's office about the property damage process.

subject_tmpl: Sidewalk buckled by tree roots — mobility concern at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I am writing about the sidewalk in front of {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The roots from the parkway tree have lifted a section of sidewalk almost three inches on one side. I use a wheelchair and I cannot safely navigate that stretch. My neighbor with a stroller also avoids it. I know there's a list for sidewalk repairs but given the accessibility issue I'm hoping this can be prioritized. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for describing the accessibility impact. We are moving your request to the top of the evaluation queue. Our engineer will assess whether a temporary ramp panel can be placed while a permanent repair is scheduled.

subject_tmpl: Parkway tree partially uprooted after heavy rain — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hello, the parkway tree in front of {ADDRESS} is partially uprooted following several days of heavy rain. The root ball on one side has lifted and the tree is visibly leaning. I'm worried it will fall on the street or the neighbor's car. This happened before my street was repaved so I think the soil got saturated differently. Can you please send someone right away? {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Can I trim the parkway tree myself? — {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Hi, quick question — the parkway tree at {ADDRESS} has a branch that extends over my porch roof and I'd like to trim it before fall. I own a pole saw and I'm comfortable with the work. Am I allowed to trim the parkway tree myself or does it need to be done by the Village? I don't want to get a ticket. Thanks, {FIRST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, parkway trees are Village property and residents may not trim them without prior authorization. Please submit a trimming request and we'll put you on the schedule. Unauthorized trimming can result in a fine.

subject_tmpl: Bark stripping on parkway tree — vandalism or disease?
body_tmpl: Good afternoon. I noticed that the parkway tree in front of {ADDRESS} has a long section of bark stripped from the trunk about four feet up from the ground. It's a clean strip roughly {AGE} inches wide. I can't tell if it was vandalism, a vehicle scrape, or some kind of disease or pest. I wanted to let the Village know either way so it can be looked at. {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Tree trimming crew parked on my lawn — {DATE}
body_tmpl: Hi, I want to file a complaint about the Village tree trimming crew that was on {STREET} on {DATE}. When they were working in front of {ADDRESS}, one of their trucks pulled partially onto my lawn and left deep ruts in the grass. Nobody knocked on my door or asked permission. I'd like to know who I should talk to about getting the lawn repaired. Thank you, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: We sincerely apologize for the damage to your lawn at {ADDRESS}, {FIRST}. Please send photos to our Public Works email and we will arrange for turf repair at no cost to you. The crew supervisor has been notified.

subject_tmpl: Tree blocking sightline — traffic safety at {STREET} & {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Frank, PD is getting complaints from patrol about the parkway tree at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS} blocking the view of oncoming traffic for drivers exiting the side street. We've had {NUM} near-miss reports in the last month. Officer Banks flagged it this morning. Can you get a crew out to clear the lower branches? Traffic safety trumps aesthetics here.
reply_tmpl: Reggie, received. I'll dispatch a crew for sight-line clearance at {STREET}/{CROSS} by end of week. Thanks for the heads up.

subject_tmpl: Request to save ash tree at {ADDRESS} — treatment options?
body_tmpl: Hello, I'm reaching out because I heard the Village may be removing the ash tree in front of {ADDRESS} due to EAB. Before it's removed, has the Village considered emamectin benzoate trunk injection treatment? I've read that it can be effective for {AGE} or more years if done before the tree loses too much canopy. This is a beautiful mature tree and I'd hate to lose it. Can we discuss? {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.

subject_tmpl: Large oak on parkway at {ADDRESS} — worried about condition after drought
body_tmpl: This is {FIRST} at {ADDRESS}. After last summer's drought, the big oak on the parkway in front of my house dropped a lot of leaves early and this spring only part of the crown leafed out. I know oaks can be slow to respond to stress but this seems more serious. Is there anyone at the Village who does routine tree health checks? I'm happy to pay for a consultation if needed but I wasn't sure if the Village has a forestry program.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for being proactive. The Village does conduct parkway tree inspections and your oak at {ADDRESS} will be added to the next assessment cycle. Drought stress in oaks can manifest for a couple of seasons — our inspector will advise on watering or treatment options.
